assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response explicitly identifies this as 'a common issue during Kubernetes upgrades' involving admission webhooks and explains that cert-manager webhook is causing pod creation failures

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Provides 'kubectl get validatingadmissionwebhook cert-manager-webhook -o yaml' command to verify webhook configuration and investigate the issue

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Clearly offers immediate solutions including 'kubectl delete validatingadmissionwebhook cert-manager-webhook' as the fastest option

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Explicitly provides 'kubectl delete validatingadmissionwebhook cert-manager-webhook' and 'kubectl delete mutatingadmissionwebhook cert-manager-webhook' commands

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Notes this is a temporary fix with 'make sure to reinstall/upgrade cert-manager as soon as possible to restore the webhook functionality' warning

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.30
assertions.reason: Includes command to 'Check current version' and mentions checking 'compatibility matrices for your operators' in prevention section

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Provides specific upgrade command 'kubectl apply -f https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/releases/download/v1.13.2/cert-manager.yaml' as permanent fix

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Option 2 specifically shows editing the webhook to change 'failurePolicy: Fail' to 'failurePolicy: Ignore' as an alternative approach

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Provides verification section with 'kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx --rm -it -- echo "success"' command to test pod creation
